protocol_name: Broth microdilution antimicrobial susceptibility testing (MIC) using dry microtiter plate
paper_source: Antimicrobial susceptibility testing (Broth microdilution method) 2025 Jul.; Rakuno Gakuen University Veterinary AMR Center, NVAL, MAFF

sequential_steps.notes: Pick a test organism from an overnight agar culture and suspend in 3 mL sterile saline; adjust turbidity to between McFarland 0.5 and 1. Specific source and destination locations are not stated.
Add 25 uL of the previously prepared bacterial suspension to 12 mL Mueller-Hinton broth to prepare inoculating bacterial suspension. Container locations are not specified.
Mix evenly after adding bacterial suspension to Mueller-Hinton broth.
Transfer inoculum into a reservoir. Exact transferred volume and deck/well positions are not stated.
Remove the dry antimicrobial microtiter plate from the refrigerator beforehand and allow it to return to room temperature before inoculation.

Inoculate the wells of the microtiter plate with 100 uL bacterial suspension using an 8- or 12-channel pipette, starting with wells with the lowest drug concentration. Avoid touching the dry antimicrobial deposited at the bottom of wells. Specific well map is not provided.

Cover the plates and incubate under culture conditions specified for each bacterial species. Stack no more than three plates. Incubation time and temperature are species-dependent and not stated in the provided source.

extraction_notes: The source describes a general broth microdilution workflow, not a fully specified Opentrons-ready plate map.

Exact microtiter plate format and manufacturer-specific Opentrons labware name are not stated; a generic 96-well flat plate and 12-channel reservoir were selected as closest Opentrons-compatible approximations.
The source mentions use of an 8- or 12-channel pipette; only a generic multichannel Opentrons pipette was listed because exact pipette capacity is not specified.
No exact well locations for reagents, inoculum reservoir, control wells, or antimicrobial concentration layout are provided, so source_location and destination_location fields are null.
Initial organism picking into 3 mL saline is described, but no pipetted transfer volume from colony to saline is stated.
Incubation temperature and duration are not given in the provided protocol sheet; they are species-specific and therefore set to null.
The antimicrobials are pre-dried in the plate wells, but the identities of the drugs tested are not specified in the provided source.
The protocol includes MIC interpretation and QC guidance, but these are not executable liquid-handling steps and were not converted into sequential robot steps.
